items.title: Psychiatrist repeatedly harassed nurse on messaging platforms

children.content: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

gallery.altText: A close-up shot of a doctor noting down symptoms of a patient on a form with a stethoscope hanging around his neck.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up shot of a doctor noting down symptoms of a patient on a form with a stethoscope hanging around his neck.

items.title: WA health official wants more support to stem 'rare' diphtheria outbreak

children.content: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

image.altText: A nurse preparing a boostrix vaccination, the vaccine's white and purple box is visible in the foreground.

items.title: Albany's hidden WWII fuel tanks transformed into global street art gallery

children.content: Tucked away in bushland on Western Australia's south coast, a hidden World War II site has become a sprawling canvas for street artists from all over the world.

image.altText: A woman looks at the tanks

items.title: Black mould and broken toilets: Inside 'dismal' government-owned homes

children.content: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

gallery.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.
A man walking towards a tin building with palm trees along the side.
A kitchen sink with broken cupboards underneath and a window above covered by dirty sheet

thumbnail.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.
A man walking towards a tin building with palm trees along the side.
A kitchen sink with broken cupboards underneath and a window above covered by dirty sheet

items.title: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare

children.content: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.

image.altText: An AFL player in black and white stripes is carried off the field on the shoulders of two others. Camera flash to the left

items.title: Collingwood champion's achievement reopens old wound outside Victoria

children.content: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.

image.altText: Craig Bradley moves the ball forward for South Australia.

items.title: 'No sign of danger': Friend details horror of fatal shark attack

children.content: The best friend of a Perth father killed by a shark off Rottnest Island says there was "absolutely no warning" before the attack.

gallery.altText: Steven Mattaboni smiling with on a boat while a young girl steers.
Liam Thomas at beach, wide shot.
Steven Mattaboni with his wife Shirene in formalwear.

thumbnail.altText: Steven Mattaboni smiling with on a boat while a young girl steers.
Liam Thomas at beach, wide shot.
Steven Mattaboni with his wife Shirene in formalwear.

items.title: Premier's fracking warning over possible gas shortfall sparks outrage

children.content: Roger Cook raises the prospect of gas fracking in WA's Kimberley region, warning the state could face future energy shortfalls if Woodside Energy's massive Browse project does not proceed.

gallery.altText: Roger Cook speaks at a press conference while wearing a high vis vest.
A close-up shot of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.
A crowd of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.

thumbnail.altText: Roger Cook speaks at a press conference while wearing a high vis vest.
A close-up shot of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.
A crowd of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.

items.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?

children.content: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

image.altText: A hand holding a phone wiht the bunbury guardian home page displayed on the screen with a pink background ebhind the phone

items.title: What's going wrong inside the City of Perth?

children.content: It's been labelled "dysfunctional" and "diabolical". What does the lord mayor have to say about the embattled City of Perth?

image.altText: Bruce Reynolds gives a subtle, toothless smile to the camera in front of council buildings.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: For Willie Rioli now is time to 'give back' to the place that gave him everything

children.content: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.

image.altText: A man sits on the grass shirtless next to other footy players. Some other men are standing in the background.

items.title: 'Live-streamed genocide': Palestinian poet writes from Gaza's frontline

children.content: The month he turned 30, Mosab Abu Toha was blindfolded, beaten and detained by the Israel Defense Forces. The essay he wrote about it won a Pulitzer Prize.

image.altText: A young Palestinian man takes a selfie in front of the bombed wreckage of buildings, under a blue sky.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship

children.content: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

image.altText: Take off of SpaceX's Starship V3

gallery.altText: People looking over the ocean watching a rocket take off

thumbnail.altText: People looking over the ocean watching a rocket take off

items.title: Police investigate triple fatal crash in Sydney's south

children.content: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney's south, the ABC understands one of the vehicles was likely a rideshare car.

image.altText: Police and fire cars parked near a crashed car.

items.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?

children.content: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

image.altText: Composite of Greens volunteers holding placards and Pauline Hanson meeting One Nation voters

items.title: Queensland man charged by anti-terrorism police

children.content: A 48-year-old has been arrested by the state's Joint Counter Terrorism Team over alleged "concerning posts" on social media.

image.altText: Uniformed AFP officers standing in a line

items.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go!'s son dies of cancer aged 14

children.content: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

image.altText: The YouTuber shared the news of his son's diagnosis in December, last year.

items.title: Toxic mouse bait forces school to shut for 'deep clean' in plague-hit town

children.content: Students of the Western Australian School of Agriculture in the WA town of Morawa are sent home and the school is closed after toxic mouse bait intended for paddocks is spread on the campus.

articles.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

image.altText: A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt

gallery.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal
A close up of some black grains of wheat.
A close up of a dozen dead mice on a shovel, Robert is wearing blue pants in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal
A close up of some black grains of wheat.
A close up of a dozen dead mice on a shovel, Robert is wearing blue pants in the background.

items.title: Death toll reaches 82 after Chinese coal mine blast

children.content: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province sits at 82, state media reports. Two people remain missing.

image.altText: A yellow school bus on a road

items.title: ASIO holiday warning did not flag elevated Hanukkah threat

children.content: Australia's intelligence agency, ASIO, issued its annual holiday threat assessment less than two weeks before the Hanukkah terrorist attack at Bondi Beach but did not identify heightened risks to the Jewish festival, despite earlier declaring antisemitism as its top priority in terms of threat to life.

image.altText: People hug at floral tribute Bondi Beach promenade

items.title: Does Queensland's premier have his attack dog on a leash?

children.content: Queensland's deputy premier made a 'dick' joke about his political opponent this week, but the difference in his approach and the premier's raises questions, writes political reporter Jack McKay.

image.altText: Jarrod Bleijie and David Crisafulli

avatar.alt: ABC Queensland state political reporter Jack McKay

items.title: Tax changes 'threaten to chill' record federal green energy support

children.content: As the federal government races towards its 2030 renewable energy goal, investors warn capital gains taxes changes risk upending the entire effort.

image.altText: A group of wind turbines dotted along a hillside against a hazy blue sky

items.title: The prickly plant powering a new spirits industry

children.content: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

gallery.altText: Close up of an agave plant.
A man working behind a bar with spirits behind him.

thumbnail.altText: Close up of an agave plant.
A man working behind a bar with spirits behind him.

items.title: IVF inquiry hears harrowing stories of fertility treatment outside big cities

children.content: Farmer and mother of two Sophie Fletcher has endured miscarriages, hours of travel and false hope throughout her IVF journey. She wants to see the system change for rural women.

image.altText: A brown haired woman with bright pink glasses stands in front of a wooden door looking seriously into camera

items.title: The perfect warm winter salad using crisp-roasted artichokes and grains

children.content: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

image.altText: A grey serving bowl of salad, with browned artichoke hearts on top, sits on a bench.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Prince, Twin Peaks and some very important donkeys

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme

children.content: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.

image.altText: Aerial shot of massive oil and gas rig in the Bass Strait off Victoria

items.title: Perth almost created a City of South Park in 2014 — here's why it might again

children.content: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.

articles.title: 'I'm over it': Minister lashes City of Perth over 'diabolical' culture

children.content: The "diabolical" City of Perth should be merged with its neighbouring council, a WA government minister has claimed, describing the "pettiness, infighting, immaturity" by elected members as "galling."

image.altText: John Carey sits with a serious contemplative expression in a TV studio. A bright beam of light shines across his face.

gallery.altText: Skyscrapers in the background with the Swan River in the foreground.

thumbnail.altText: Skyscrapers in the background with the Swan River in the foreground.
